title: Ardisia esquipulasana
intro: Ardisia esquipulasana, also known as the "Oreganillo", is a plant species native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Myrsinaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and gastrointestinal disorders. Bioactive constituents of Ardisia esquipulasana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ardisia esquipulasana is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used to make infusions and decoctions, often used to treat a variety of health issues.
taxonomy: Ardisia esquipulasana is classified within the family Primulaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Ardisia. This plant is native to Central America and has been identified as a species of evergreen shrub or small tree. The classification of Ardisia esquipulasana is further refined within the subfamily Myrsinoideae. The species is characterized by its small, white flowers and red or purple berries.
